medical_history.history_of_present_illness: increasing facial pressure and occasional purulent nasal discharge. Denied pain, numbness, epistaxis, or visual changes.
medical_history.past_medical_history: incidental finding of an intranasal mass on computed tomography (CT) performed after a motor vehicle accident 3 years prior to his presentation. This mass was untreated. He also reported resection of a mass of unknown pathology from the base of his tongue during childhood.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: mass visible in the left nasal vestibule on anterior rhinoscopy. A neurologic exam was normal except for a subjective decreased sense of smell. Extraocular muscle motion and vision were normal. A neck examination revealed no abnormal findings.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Frozen sections taken from the posterior ethmoids revealed a small focus of carcinoma-in-situ (CIS) amid a large quantity of tissue consistent with verrucous carcinoma (VC).

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Extensive sampling of the submitted tumor
laboratory_examinations.findings: Demonstrated a predominantly exophytic or papillary lesion composed of well-differentiated, cytologically bland, squamous epithelium with extensive parakeratosis and absent stratum granulosum. The base of the lesion showed rounded or bulbous rete pegs with broad pushing borders and an intact basement membrane. No other areas of epithelial dysplasia, CIS, or invasion were noted in the thoroughly sampled specimen.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Incidental finding of an intranasal mass.
Showed a destructive lesion apparently originating in the left posterior ethmoid, with extensive erosion of the anterior skull base and the right and left lamina papyracea.

Showed an intranasal soft-tissue mass isointense with brain on T1 imaging. This mass enhanced with contrast. While the tumor entered the pterygopalatine fossa, there was no clear ganglion or infraorbital nerve involvement. Right orbital involvement was limited to erosion of the lateral orbital wall. Left orbital involvement included obliteration of the nasolacrimal duct and invasion into the tissues beneath the medial canthus. While the tumor contacted the medial rectus, the interface with the muscle was smooth. Intraconal and optic nerve involvement were not present. In the anterior cranial fossa, a 3-cm cribriform defect was noted. While dural enhancement was present, the dura/tumor interface was smooth. Imaging of the brain was otherwise normal. The neck showed no lymphadenopathy.
Reveal the extent of the surgical resection. No radiographic evidence of recurrent disease is noted.

other_tests.findings: Significant for a mass visible in the left nasal vestibule on anterior rhinoscopy. A neurologic exam was normal except for a subjective decreased sense of smell. Extraocular muscle motion and vision were normal. A neck examination revealed no abnormal findings.
Performed in the operating room after a non-diagnostic clinic biopsy.
